explanation: This command creates a one-time scheduled task using wscript.exe at 00:00, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It includes all required elements: uncommon action (wscript), one-time schedule, and time at 00:00.

This command uses cscript.exe in a one-time scheduled task at 00:00, which aligns with the Sigma rule's detection logic. It includes equivalent syntax and structure to avoid false negatives in testing.
This command creates a one-time task using PowerShell at 00:00, which is flagged by the Sigma rule. It serves as a test case to validate detection of scheduled tasks with suspicious actions.

output: schtasks /create /tn TestTask /tr "wmic process call create "notepad.exe"" /sc once /st 00:00
explanation: This command uses wmic.exe in a one-time task at 00:00, matching the Sigma rule's criteria. It demonstrates how wmic can be used in scheduled tasks for testing detection.
